reasons: Baxia directly reduces Alpha's healing value, which is central to Alpha's extended-fight pattern.
Baxia can contest Alpha without giving easy burst access to the backline.

proof.summary: Alpha wants to heal during extended fights, while Baxia reduces healing and shield effects to make those fights less favorable.
worksBestWhen: Baxia applies pressure before Alpha's sustain skill connects on multiple targets.
The team focuses Alpha while his sustain is reduced.
failureCases: Baxia arrives late after Alpha has already healed.
Alpha avoids Baxia and dives softer targets.

reasons: Valir can keep Alpha away with knockback and slows.
Alpha is much less threatening when he cannot keep enemies inside his skill range.

proof.summary: Alpha wants to stay attached in melee range, while Valir can repeatedly disengage and slow his approach.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Alpha's engage.
failureCases: Alpha flanks Valir before Valir can react.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Alpha before Alpha's sustain cycle becomes stable.
Defense reduction and strong early trading can force Alpha into losing short trades.

proof.summary: Alpha prefers longer fights, while Dyrroth can shorten the trade with defense-reduction burst before Alpha stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth plays aggressively before Alpha gets durability items.
Dyrroth dodges Alpha's key sustain skill.
failureCases: Dyrroth extends the fight after missing burst.
Alpha lands multiple sustain skills in a grouped fight.

reasons: Karrie can punish Alpha's durable frontline pattern with repeated true-damage marks.
Alpha struggles when he cannot reach or burst Karrie before she stacks damage.

proof.summary: Alpha wants durability and sustain to carry fights, while Karrie's true damage pressures him through that durability.
worksBestWhen: Karrie fights after core attack-speed items.
failureCases: Alpha dives Karrie before she can stack marks.
Karrie has no protection from flank engage.

reasons: Lunox can threaten durable fighters with penetration and burst timing.
Alpha is vulnerable if Lunox punishes during his cooldown window instead of letting him sustain.

proof.summary: Alpha wants extended healing trades, while Lunox can use penetration and burst timing to punish him before the sustain loop resets.
worksBestWhen: Lunox keeps distance and waits for Alpha's engage skill.
Lunox has protection from frontline allies.
failureCases: Lunox uses burst while Alpha still has full sustain tools and teammates nearby.
